ollama_format.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

response.clean_text: { "response_text": "In the provided dataset, the number of households employing the E-RO strategy (non-learning agents) exhibits notable variability across simulation runs, despite homogeneous behavioral configurations. The counts fluctuate between 0 and 41 households over unnumbered time steps. Initial counts in most simulations (e.g., 20, 23, 25 households) align with the baseline E-RO contingent setup. However, average E-RO counts decline by ~8–35% in many runs, with late-stage declines in several simulations (e.g., to 0, 2, or 4 households) suggesting potential strategy abandonment or extinction under specific conditions. Climatic variability (global rain variability = 100) may accelerate these shifts, as rainfall fluctuations often precede drops in E-RO persistence. In 8 runs, counts stabilize at 15–25 households, while 11 runs see near-complete E-RO attrition. These patterns highlight sensitivity to resource-scarcity thresholds (satisficing-threshold = 0) and social learning mechanisms implicitly interacting with static E-RO populations." }

parsed_response.response_text: In the provided dataset, the number of households employing the E-RO strategy (non-learning agents) exhibits notable variability across simulation runs, despite homogeneous behavioral configurations. The counts fluctuate between 0 and 41 households over unnumbered time steps. Initial counts in most simulations (e.g., 20, 23, 25 households) align with the baseline E-RO contingent setup. However, average E-RO counts decline by ~8–35% in many runs, with late-stage declines in several simulations (e.g., to 0, 2, or 4 households) suggesting potential strategy abandonment or extinction under specific conditions. Climatic variability (global rain variability = 100) may accelerate these shifts, as rainfall fluctuations often precede drops in E-RO persistence. In 8 runs, counts stabilize at 15–25 households, while 11 runs see near-complete E-RO attrition. These patterns highlight sensitivity to resource-scarcity thresholds (satisficing-threshold = 0) and social learning mechanisms implicitly interacting with static E-RO populations.

In 8 runs, counts stabilize at 15–25 households, while 11 runs see near-complete E-RO attrition.
